lessons.title: Hello, Lua!
lessons.summary: Print your first message in Lua.

payload.title: Lua is used in Roblox!
payload.body: Lua is the scripting language inside Roblox Studio. print() sends text to the output window — it works exactly the same way in Roblox scripts.

payload.prompt: What does print() do in Lua?
payload.explanation: print() outputs text — super useful for debugging your Roblox scripts!
Text strings go inside quotes in Lua.

lessons.title: Local Variables
lessons.summary: Store values with the local keyword.

payload.title: local keeps things tidy
payload.body: In Lua (and Roblox), you almost always use local when creating a variable. It keeps the variable contained so other scripts can't accidentally change it.

payload.prompt: Why use local before a variable in Lua?
options: It keeps the variable contained to the script
It sends the variable to the server
payload.explanation: local scope prevents your variable from leaking into other scripts.

payload.prompt: Declare a local variable called coins with value 10.
payload.explanation: Lua uses local — not var or let like other languages.

payload.title: Lua handles math easily
payload.body: Lua supports +, -, *, / just like a calculator. You can store the result in a variable and print it.
payload.codeSample:
local apples = 10
local eaten = 3
local left = apples - eaten
print(left) -- prints 7

payload.prompt: What symbol divides two numbers in Lua?
payload.explanation: / is the division operator in Lua (and almost every language).
* is the multiplication operator in Lua.

lessons.title: If Statements
lessons.summary: Make decisions with if / then / end.

payload.title: if / then / end
payload.body: Lua if statements end with the keyword end instead of a closing brace. This makes the code look clean and readable.
payload.codeSample:
local health = 50
if health < 100 then
  print("Need healing!")
end

payload.prompt: How does Lua close an if block?
payload.explanation: Lua uses end to close if, for, while, and function blocks.
After the condition, Lua requires the keyword then.

lessons.title: For Loops
lessons.summary: Repeat code a set number of times.

payload.title: Count with a for loop
payload.body: A Lua for loop counts from a start number to an end number. Great for spawning enemies, filling inventories, or animating repeated actions.
payload.codeSample:
for i = 1, 5 do
  print("Wave " .. i)
end

payload.prompt: How many times does 'for i = 1, 3 do' loop?
payload.explanation: It loops for i = 1, 2, 3 — exactly 3 times.

payload.prompt: Complete the for loop to count from 1 to 10.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.summary: Wrap reusable code in a function.

payload.title: Define and call functions
payload.body: Functions let you write code once and call it many times. In Roblox scripts, almost everything you write lives inside a function.
payload.codeSample:
local function greet(name)
  print("Hi, " .. name .. "!")
end

greet("Player")

payload.prompt: What keyword starts a function in Lua?
payload.explanation: Lua uses function — followed by the name and parentheses.

payload.prompt: Start a function definition called attack.
payload.explanation: local function is the Lua pattern for a scoped function definition.
